summary: This lecture covers the third obligation of wudu (ablution): wiping the head. The speaker explains that wiping must be done over the entirety of the head, starting from where face washing ends and moving backward. The ears are considered part of the head and must be wiped without taking additional water. The speaker details the Prophet's methodology, citing various hadith narrations about how he performed this act. The lecture also discusses scholarly differences regarding the minimum requirement for head wiping, clarifying that while scholars debate the bare minimum (ranging from any part of the head to one-fourth), they all agree the Prophet wiped his entire head when it was uncovered.
suggested_title: The Third Obligation of Wudu: Wiping the Head and Ears
catchy_title: How did the Prophet wipe his head in wudu?
hadith_chapter: Chapter on the Obligations of Wudu
